---
title: "Generative Additive Model"
author: ',Chatty'
date: "2026-03-14"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```
```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(mgcv)
```

```{r}
library(readr)
df <- read_csv("C:/Users/lamin/Downloads/crime_prediction_dataset.csv")
df
```



```{r}
str(df)
```
```{r}
# --- STEP 1: DEFINE THE GRID BOUNDARIES ---
# Your coordinates are masked (between 0 and 1). We will create a 20x20 grid.
lon_breaks <- seq(0, 1, length.out = 11) 
lat_breaks <- seq(0, 1, length.out = 11)

# Time is in hours (0 to 24). We will create 24 1-hour intervals.
time_breaks <- seq(0, 24, by = 1)
```

```{r}

# Discretize the raw data into bins
df_binned <- df %>%
  mutate(
    lon_bin = cut(masked_longitude, breaks = num_lon_bins, labels = FALSE),
    lat_bin = cut(masked_latitude, breaks = num_lat_bins, labels = FALSE),
    time_bin = cut(time_of_crime, breaks = time_breaks, labels = FALSE, include.lowest = TRUE)
  )
```


```{r}
# Get the midpoints of the bins to use as features in the GAM
lon_midpoints <- seq(min(df$masked_longitude), max(df$masked_longitude), length.out = num_lon_bins)
lat_midpoints <- seq(min(df$masked_latitude), max(df$masked_latitude), length.out = num_lat_bins)
time_midpoints <- seq(0.5, 23.5, by = 1)

# Get the center points of these grids so the GAM has continuous numbers to work with
lon_centers <- head(lon_breaks, -1) + diff(lon_breaks)/2
lat_centers <- head(lat_breaks, -1) + diff(lat_breaks)/2
time_centers <- head(time_breaks, -1) + diff(time_breaks)/2

```


```{r}
# --- STEP 2: MAP RAW DATA TO THE GRID (Equation: s_i, t_j) ---
df_binned <- df %>%
  mutate(
    # Map masked_longitude and masked_latitude to grid indices (1 to 20)
    grid_x_index = as.numeric(cut(masked_longitude, breaks = lon_breaks, include.lowest = TRUE)),
    grid_y_index = as.numeric(cut(masked_latitude, breaks = lat_breaks, include.lowest = TRUE)),
    
    # Map time_of_crime to time indices (1 to 24)
    time_index = as.numeric(cut(time_of_crime, breaks = time_breaks, include.lowest = TRUE))
  ) %>%
  
    # Remove any NA values in case a data point falls exactly outside the 0-1 or 0-24 boundaries
  drop_na(grid_x_index, grid_y_index, time_index)

# Aggregate to find where crimes ACTUALLY happened (incident = 1)
actual_crimes <- df_binned %>%
  group_by(grid_x_index, grid_y_index, day_of_crime, time_index) %>%
  summarise(crime_count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(incident = 1) 
```


```{r}
# --- STEP 3: CREATE THE BACKGROUND (0s) ---
# We must generate all possible grid/time combinations to represent places/times with NO crime
all_days <- unique(df$day_of_crime)

# expand_grid creates every possible combination of location and time
full_grid <- expand_grid(
  grid_x_index = 1:20,
  grid_y_index = 1:20,
  day_of_crime = all_days,
  time_index = 1:24
)

# Merge actual crimes onto the full grid. If a cell has no crime, incident = 0
model_data <- full_grid %>%
  left_join(actual_crimes, by = c("grid_x_index", "grid_y_index", "day_of_crime", "time_index")) %>%
  mutate(
    incident = replace_na(incident, 0), # 1 if crime happened, 0 if not
    
    # Assign the physical center coordinates back to the grid for the GAM to use
    grid_longitude = lon_centers[grid_x_index],
    grid_latitude  = lat_centers[grid_y_index],
    grid_time      = time_centers[time_index]
  )
```


```{r}
# --- STEP 4: FIT THE GAM MODEL ---
# We predict the probability of 'incident' (0 or 1) using the grid variables
gam_model <- gam(
  incident ~ te(grid_longitude, grid_latitude, k = 5) +  # 2D Spatial Hot Spot
             s(day_of_crime, k = 5) +                     # Day trend
             s(grid_time, bs = "cc", k = 5),             # Time of day (Cyclic)
  family = binomial(link = "logit"),
  data = model_data,
  method = "REML"
)
```

```{r}
summary(gam_model)
```
